description: Hospital discharge summary — post-surgical right total hip arthroplasty, 3-day inpatient stay, home with PT
parsed_text:
DISCHARGE SUMMARY
St. Augustine Medical Center
Department: Orthopedic Surgery — Inpatient
Encounter ID: SAMC-DISCH-2025-088341
Admission Date: 2025-10-28
Discharge Date: 2025-10-31
Date of Note: 2025-10-31

Provider: Dr. Kevin Larsen, MD
NPI: 5544332211
Specialty: Orthopedic Surgery

Patient: Martinez, Rosa E.
DOB: 1948-05-22
MRN: SAMC-MRN-00762103

Facility: St. Augustine Medical Center
Department: Orthopedic Surgery — Inpatient

---
CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR ADMISSION
Elective right total hip arthroplasty for end-stage osteoarthritis.

SUBJECTIVE
77-year-old female admitted for elective right total hip replacement. History of right hip OA progressive over 5 years, failed conservative management (PT, NSAIDs, corticosteroid injections). Pre-operative clearance obtained from PCP and cardiology. Patient was ambulatory with a cane pre-operatively. Pre-op vitals stable. Pertinent PMH: HTN, hypothyroidism, osteoporosis.

OBJECTIVE (Discharge Vitals)
  BP: 128/74 mmHg   HR: 66 bpm   Temp: 99.0°F   RR: 16 breaths/min
  SpO2: 96% on room air   Weight: 158 lbs

Hospital Course:
  10/28: Right total hip arthroplasty performed under spinal anesthesia without intraoperative complications. Estimated blood loss 220 mL. Cefazolin perioperative antibiotic prophylaxis given.
  10/29: PT out of bed to chair, standing with walker. Pain controlled on acetaminophen and oxycodone PRN.
  10/30: Ambulated 50 feet with walker. Occupational therapy evaluated for ADL modifications.
  10/31: Ambulating 100 feet, tolerating oral diet, voiding, afebrile. Ready for discharge.
  Post-op X-ray (10/29): Hardware in good position, no acute complications.

ASSESSMENT
1. Status post right total hip arthroplasty (Z96.641) — Uncomplicated, appropriate recovery.
2. Primary osteoarthritis, right hip (M16.11) — Surgical indication, addressed.
3. Essential hypertension (I10) — Chronic, continued home medications.
4. Hypothyroidism (E03.9) — Chronic, continued levothyroxine.
5. Osteoporosis without current pathological fracture (M81.0) — Chronic, Vitamin D/calcium continued.

PLAN / DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS
1. Discharge to home with home health physical therapy (3x/week x 4 weeks) and home health nursing (1x/week x 2 weeks).
2. Hip precautions: No hip flexion > 90°, no internal rotation, no crossing legs. Hip precaution instructions reviewed with patient and daughter.
3. Medications at discharge: Aspirin 81 mg daily (DVT prophylaxis x 35 days), oxycodone 5 mg q6h PRN pain (10-day supply), acetaminophen 650 mg q6h scheduled, levothyroxine 50 mcg daily, lisinopril 5 mg daily, calcium carbonate 600 mg + Vitamin D3 1000 IU BID.
4. Wound care: Keep incision dry x 72 hours. Staple removal at follow-up.
5. Return precautions: ER for fever > 101.5°F, increased redness/drainage at incision, sudden severe hip pain, leg swelling, or shortness of breath.
6. Follow-up: Orthopedic surgery office in 2 weeks for wound check and staple removal.

Electronically signed: Dr. Kevin Larsen, MD — 2025-10-31 11:20
